content.full_text:
McCUTCHEN, DOYLE, BROWN & ENERSEN
COUNSELORS AT LAW
1331 NORTH CALIFORNIA BOULEVARD
POST OFFICE BOX V
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596

SAN FRANCISCO

LOS ANGELES

SAN JOSE

WALNUT CREEK

WASHINGTON, D.C

TAIPEI

AFFILIATED OFFICE

BANGKOK

TELEPHONE (510) 937-8000

FACSIMILE (510) 975-5390

DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

(510) 975-5318

September 22, 1992

Linda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 610
Sacramento, CA 95814

FPPC - Lobbying

Dear Ms. Cassady:

This letter serves to confirm the informal advice you
have provided me. Please let me know if in any respect I have
misunderstood your advice.

1. Litigation is exempt from the definition of
lobbying. Therefore, all time that is undertaken in litigation
need not be reported.

2. Neither LAFCO nor SCAQMD are state agencies for
purposes of the lobbying reporting obligations.

3. If a partner of a law firm has a $10,000 draw in
one month, and she works 30% of her time in a rate making
proceeding on "administrative testimony" (as that term is
defined in Reg. 18239) and 5% of her time on "direct
communication" (as that term is defined in Reg. 18239), she has
not crossed the $2,000 threshold of Regulation 12239(b) because
the only time counted toward that threshold is the 5% of
"direct communication." In this hypothetical, the 5% of her
time translates into $500. Moreover, the law firm of which she
is a partner will count only the $500 of "direct communication"
time towards the threshold of determining if the firm is a
lobbying firm.

I thank you for your prompt and courteous assistance.

Very truly yours,

Palmer Brown Madden
